captures.notes: Canonical Codrops tutorial link for attribution and technical framing; local evidence below is generated from scratch for learning review.
Local generated rebuild screenshot showing readable MSDF-like text, noise dissolve edge, glyph fragments, dust particles, petal vectors, and bloom proxy; not copied from the Codrops demo.
Local generated motion clip isolates typography dissolve timing, dust release, petal drift, and bloom emphasis; not copied from the Codrops demo.
Standalone local HTML/SVG exercise reconstructing the primitive with generated glyph fragments, dust particles, petal vectors, dissolve band, and CSS-only motion.
Manifest lists only generated local learning evidence plus source-link attribution boundaries.

payload.dissolve_state: word stays readable while its outgoing edge breaks into glyph fragments
payload.rebuild_scope: local generated typography study from the public tutorial concept, not a capture of upstream font geometry
payload.text_volume_model: bold word face, warm chromatic shadows, and rectangular glyph fragments imply a signed-distance text material

modules.review_notes: Complete for first-pass learning: still image, motion clip, and HTML note all represent the same typography-to-debris primitive.

payload.motion_model: dust samples and petal vectors move on separate arcs from the dissolve edge
payload.visual_role: separates weak evidence decay from salient evidence bursts instead of using one generic scatter field

modules.review_notes: Particle vector layer is explicit enough to teach debris taxonomy and release timing separately from the text material.

payload.bloom_proxy: pink and gold halos approximate selective bloom/MRT emphasis without claiming the upstream renderer
payload.source_boundary: does not claim Codrops shader code, TSL nodes, MRT setup, or exact animation curves

modules.review_notes: Material notes are complete for teaching shader-material anatomy while keeping upstream implementation claims bounded.

payload.runtime_target: offline SVG/CSS review exercise; production analogue would use Three.js WebGPU and TSL

modules.review_notes: Asset pipeline is represented as a clean split between text surface, fragments, dust, petals, and bloom proxy.

payload.evidence_layer: local PNG proves dissolve state, local GIF proves debris release timing, HTML note proves the rebuild recipe
payload.learning_primitive: claim label dissolving into evidence particles
payload.review_gap: canonical tutorial remains link-only; local rebuild teaches mechanics without upstream media
payload.route_decision: use readable text for stable claims, dissolve threshold for uncertainty, dust for weak evidence decay, petals for salient evidence bursts, and bloom for attention
payload.source_trail: canonical tutorial plus generated local reconstruction
payload.uncertainty: exact upstream WebGPU/TSL/MRT implementation is not claimed

modules.review_notes: VULCA mapping is complete because source, evidence, review gap, route decision, and uncertainty are all represented.

summary: 2026 MSDF text dissolve tutorial rebuilt as a local typography, shader-material, dust, and petal-particle learning primitive with still, motion, code, and asset evidence.
title: WebGPU Gommage Effect
why_relevant: Strong teaching case for converting readable technical labels into shader-driven debris states, useful for review uncertainty and evidence decay metaphors.
